claim: Treat Claude Code like a junior engineer; QA testing (not coding) becomes the bottleneck when using agent coding assistants, consuming 95% of time
actionable: Optimize for testing and validation, not coding speed; build rigorous QA testing workflows that protect your design intent
source_title: 10 Months of Unity Dev with Claude Code

claim: Protecting your design intent is critical with agentic tools; provide feedback on deviations immediately so the agent stays aligned with your vision
actionable: After each feature, validate against your design intent and give specific feedback on deviations before moving to the next task
source_title: 10 Months of Unity Dev with Claude Code

claim: The workflow matters less than having a clear vision; Claude Code adapts to your workflow if you have a specific target user experience defined upfront
actionable: Don't copy other people's workflows; define your own clear design intent first, then let the agent adapt to your style
source_title: 10 Months of Unity Dev with Claude Code

claim: Agentic coding feels like art (gestural, iterative, copying mastery) rather than traditional programming; non-programmers may have an advantage with visual thinking
actionable: If you're a visual thinker or artist, your workflow may be better suited to agent coding than traditional text-based programming
source_title: 10 Months of Unity Dev with Claude Code

claim: Monthly subscription plan ($100) allows 8-10 hours per day of agentic coding without scaling to API usage costs; cost-predictability is critical for indie development
actionable: Use predictable subscription plans for agentic development to reduce cost uncertainty; only scale to usage-based pricing when confident
source_title: 10 Months of Unity Dev with Claude Code
